title: polypogon fugax
intro: Polypogon fugax, also known as the wandering grass or common hair grass, is a species of grass native to Europe and Western Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Polypogon fugax include relieving symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, as well as reducing inflammation and soothing skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Polypogon fugax often involve infusing the plant in water or oil to create teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally.
